title: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma with secondary TP53-mutated MDS in a 75-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: Increasing fatigue and worsening cytopenia in a patient with history of multiple myeloma and recent radical prostatectomy
patient.vital_signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP 128/74 mmHg, HR 92/min (regular), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 96% (room air).

1.setting: Outpatient hematology clinic (follow-up of a high-risk myeloma patient with new cytopenias)
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with a history of multiple myeloma and recent solid tumor surgery. You can review history and physical examination, obtain routine laboratory tests, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging and specialist consultations. Decisions about further invasive diagnostics or treatment changes are based on these findings.

2.setting: Hematology day unit / inpatient ward (evaluation of persistent cytopenias and bone disease)
2.capabilities: The patient is undergoing extended evaluation for persistent cytopenias and skeletal symptoms. You can organize bone marrow assessment, targeted imaging, and additional laboratory and virologic investigations. Multidisciplinary input is available. Some specialized results may still be pending.

3.setting: Specialist myeloma/MDS conference (combined plasma cell and myeloid disease management)
3.capabilities: You now have access to histology, cytogenetics, and molecular data from both plasma cell and non–plasma cell compartments, as well as a complete treatment history. You are expected to integrate these findings with comorbidities and risk scores to define final diagnoses, risk stratification, and a realistic treatment and supportive care strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for age, sex, comorbidities, social situation, family history, or anthropometric data.
demographics.content:
Age: 75 years
Sex: Male
Comorbidities: Dilated cardiomyopathy with moderately reduced systolic function, suspected cardiotoxic versus amyloid etiology. Chronic kidney disease stage 2. Ventricular extrasystoles. Arterial hypertension. Hypercholesterolemia. Type 2 diabetes mellitus. History of vasovagal syncope. Right inguinal hernia repair (1998). Lumbar disc prolapse at L4–L5. Bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome. Prostate carcinoma (Gleason 3 + 4 = 7, pT2cN0, PSA 5.4 ng/mL at diagnosis) treated with radical prostatectomy (R0) via da Vinci system in 2025-08. Anxiety disorder. Severe depressive episode (2019). History of multiple myeloma.
Social/Family: Married, three adult children (two daughters, one son), six grandchildren; supportive family network. Brother died of myocardial infarction at 61; sister with type 2 diabetes.
Height: 169 cm
Weight: 102 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medications.
history_physical.content:
2025-09-13: The patient presented to the outpatient clinic. He had a recent history of organ-confined prostate carcinoma (Gleason 3 + 4 = 7, pT2cN0) treated with radical prostatectomy in 2025-08, with an appropriate postoperative PSA decline (0.06 ng/mL at the time of presentation). CT staging showed no evidence of residual or metastatic disease. He reported increasing fatigue over the preceding several weeks but remained able to perform daily activities. There was no exertional dyspnea. He described pallor and noted worsening cytopenia on recent laboratory tests. An additional complaint was urinary incontinence since prostatectomy, requiring daily pad use, which he found socially limiting. He denied fever, chills, or night sweats. Appetite was preserved, and weight remained stable. His psychiatric history of depression and anxiety was stable; however, he expressed frustration regarding reduced stamina and persistent incontinence.
Current medication: acyclovir 400 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg Mo/Wed/Fri, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, ramipril 2.5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, sitagliptin 100 mg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, dapagliflozin 10 mg 1-0-0.

cbc_chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count, differential, coagulation, serum chemistry, and cardiac/metabolic markers.
cbc_chemistry.content:
Hb 8.1 g/dL, WBC 3.2 ×10⁹/L, Plt 33 ×10⁹/L; MCV 98 fL, MCH 34 pg; differential: neutrophils 38%, lymphocytes 42%, monocytes 17%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 sec, INR 1.1.
Chemistry: Na 133 mmol/L, K 4.8 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL, BUN 29 mg/dL, AST 48 U/L, ALT 74 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL, LDH 319 U/L, uric acid 6.6 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.10 mmol/L, CRP 1.8 mg/dL, PCT <0.1 ng/mL.
NT-proBNP 550 pg/mL, troponin T 0.0 ng/mL.
HbA1c 8.2%, blood glucose 181 mg/dL.

ct_skeleton.valid_request_if: Requests for skeletal imaging or CT skeleton findings.
ct_skeleton.content: 2025-09-15: CT skeleton showed several osteolytic foci persisting in the thoracic spine and pelvis, consistent with known myeloma-related bone disease. New vertebral compression fractures were observed at T7, T9, and T11, with up to 30% loss of vertebral body height. Fracture margins appeared sharp, consistent with recent occurrence. Posterior wall involvement could not be excluded, especially at T9 and T11, where cortical irregularity was visible. Old compression fractures at T5 and T12 showed reparative changes.

echo_abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for echocardiography and abdominal ultrasound findings.
echo_abdominal_ultrasound.content:
Echocardiography demonstrated dilated cardiomyopathy with mildly reduced systolic function (LVEF 46%), without significant valvular abnormalities or pericardial effusion.
Abdominal ultrasound showed a post-prostatectomy bed without residual mass. The kidneys were normal in size and morphology, without hydronephrosis. Liver and spleen were normal with homogeneous parenchymal texture. No lymphadenopathy was identified. Pancreas, gallbladder, and biliary system were unremarkable. No free abdominal fluid was present.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow aspirate, biopsy, blast percentage, or plasma-cell burden.
bone_marrow.content:
Cytology: The aspirate smear was hypocellular for age (15%), showing markedly reduced trilineage hematopoiesis with dysplastic features in both erythroid and granulocytic lineages. Erythropoiesis displayed nuclear irregularities, multinuclearity, and occasional megaloblastoid forms. Granulopoiesis showed hypogranular myelocytes and pseudo–Pelger like neutrophils. Megakaryopoiesis was decreased, with a predominance of micromegakaryocytes and separated nuclei. Plasma cells were slightly increased, with a small atypical population (< 5%) showing eccentric nuclei, basophilic cytoplasm, and occasional perinuclear clearing. A focal blast component (around 5%) was present, composed of medium-sized cells with a high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, open chromatin, and indistinct nucleoli.
Flow cytometry: An expanded but small monotypic plasma-cell population (<1%) was identified, expressing CD38⁺, CD138⁺, cytoplasmic κ light-chain restriction, aberrant CD56 positivity, and CD19 negativity. A blast population (4%) was detected, CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and negative for lymphoid markers (CD3⁻, CD19⁻, CD4⁻, CD8⁻, CD20⁻), consistent with myelodysplastic-type blasts. The T-cell compartment showed a normal polytypic pattern, and the NK-cell fraction was preserved.
Histology: The trephine biopsy was hypocellular (15–20%) for age, with patchy hematopoietic activity and interstitial infiltration by atypical plasma cells (5%) showing irregular nuclei and mild atypia, arranged in small focal clusters. Trilineage hematopoiesis was markedly reduced, with dysplastic erythroid and myeloid maturation and decreased megakaryopoiesis with small, hypolobated forms. CD34 immunostaining highlighted scattered blast cells (5%) in focal groups. Reticulin staining revealed mild diffuse fibrosis (MF-1).

cytogenetics_fish.valid_request_if: Requests for cytogenetic karyotype or FISH results associated with myeloid or plasma-cell disease.
cytogenetics_fish.content:
Karyotype: 46,XY,der(5)t(5;19)(q11;?p11),+7,r(7)(p1q11)x2,t(8;8)(q22;q24),der(12;19)(q10;?q10),t(13;17)(q21;q24)[18].
FISH: Showed gain of 1q and 5p, trisomy 3, tetrasomy 9 and 11, gain of 8q, and deletions of 17p and 5q.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for spinal MRI, virology, immunologic status, or myeloma protein studies.
extended_diagnostics.content:
MRI holospinal: Confirmed acute/subacute compression fractures at T7, T9, and T11 (hypointense T1 / hyperintense STIR signal). Posterior wall irregularities were present at T9 and T11, without significant retropulsion or spinal canal compromise. There was no spinal stenosis or epidural mass, and the spinal cord showed normal signal intensity without intramedullary lesion.
Urology consultation: Post-prostatectomy status was reviewed. Clinical examination and urinalysis showed bland urine without hematuria, proteinuria, or infection. Persistent urinary incontinence was expected postoperatively; pelvic-floor training was recommended.
Virology from bone-marrow aspirate: EBV DNA was positive (3 920 copies/mL). Parvovirus B19, VZV, and HSV-1/2 were negative.
Peripheral blood: CMV PCR was negative; EBV PCR was positive at a lower copy number (< 250 copies/mL).
Immunologic status: CD4⁺ T cells 320/µL, CD8⁺ T cells 720/µL.
Serum free light chains: κ 21 mg/L, λ 12 mg/L; κ/λ ratio 1.75.
Immunoglobulins: IgG 790 mg/dL, IgA 68 mg/dL, IgM 41 mg/dL.
Immunofixation: Residual monoclonal IgG-κ trace band was detectable.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for next-generation sequencing results in the non–plasma-cell compartment.
molecular_panel.content: CD138 negative fraction: Mutations in TP53 (1.: exon 4, c.273G>A, p.Trp91*(VAF 6%); 2.: exon 8, c.893_901delinsT, p.Glu298Valfs*5 (VAF 18%)). No Mutation detected in ASXL1, RUNX1, CEBPA, FLT3, IDH1, IDH2, NPM1, NRAS, KRAS, SRSF2, SF3B1, U2AF1, ZRSR2, STAG2, EZH2, JAK2, MPL, CALR, KIT, WT1, PHF6, PTPN11, BCOR, BCORL1.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for current disease status of myeloma and secondary myeloid disease, and markers suitable for monitoring.
mrd_status.content: The multiple myeloma clone persisted with traceable cytogenetic aberrations and M-Protein. A TP53 mutation was found in the CD138 negative fraction, most likely representing secondary myeloid disease.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for detailed myeloma treatment history including prior transplants and CAR-T, and timing of TP53-mutated disease.
treatment_history.content:
Initial diagnosis (2020-04): Multiple myeloma IgG-κ, stage IIIA (Salmon & Durie), stage II (R-ISS).
CRAB criteria: anemia, osteolytic lesions, displaced rib fractures (4th/5th right), diffuse axial skeletal infiltration. Baseline paraprotein: IgG 7379 mg/dL, κ FLC 265 mg/L, Bence–Jones proteinuria.
Bone marrow (2020-04-13): 70% plasma cells. Cytogenetics revealed gains of 1q, 5p, trisomy 3, tetrasomy 9 and 11, gain 8q.
First-line therapy (2020-04–07): Dexamethasone pulse for 4 days (from 2020-04-18), followed by bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² SC d1,4,8,11, cyclophosphamide 900 mg/m² IV d1 q21, and dexamethasone 40 mg PO d1–4, 9–12, 17–20 for four cycles, achieving partial remission (IgG to 3028 mg/dL).
Stem cell mobilization (2020-07): Cyclophosphamide 2 g/m² d−3 & d−2 and etoposide 100 mg/m² d−3 to d−1, followed by apheresis (2020-08, yield: 7.1 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg).
2020-08–09: Daratumumab 16 mg/kg IV weekly, lenalidomide 25 mg PO d1–21, and dexamethasone 40 mg weekly. Initial partial remission (2020-08-17 IgG 1481 mg/dL), followed by disease progression (2020-08-29 IgG 2875 mg/dL).
2020-10-12: Elotuzumab 10 mg/kg IV d1,8,15, pomalidomide 4 mg PO d1–21, dexamethasone 40 mg weekly, achieving partial remission (2020-12-13 IgG 1147 mg/dL).
Autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT #1): High-dose melphalan 100 mg/m² d−3 and d−2 (2020-12-21) followed by stem-cell infusion on 2020-12-23 (4.0 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg). Engraftment: neutrophils > 1.0 × 10⁹/L and platelets > 20 × 10⁹/L on 2021-01-01. Result: VGPR achieved.
Second ASCT (2021-02): High-dose melphalan 200 mg/m², stem-cell infusion on 2021-02-08. Complicated by ICU admission for sepsis and respiratory failure due to RSV pneumonia, requiring intubation and mechanical ventilation. Acute renal failure (creatinine peak 3.1 mg/dL) occurred and the patient required hemodialysis. Empiric antibiotics: meropenem 1 g q8h for 12 days and linezolid 600 mg q12h for 10 days. During endotracheal tube exchange, a tracheal injury occurred, necessitating tracheostomy (2021-02-25). The patient developed critical illness polyneuropathy, requiring prolonged rehabilitation. Tracheostoma closure: 2021-03-25 after recovery. Engraftment: neutrophils (2021-02-17), platelets (2021-02-18). Serological CR achieved after second autoSCT.
Maintenance (2021-05–2024-11): Ixazomib 4 mg PO d1,8,15 per 28-day cycle. From early 2022, immunofixation became positive again, but the patient remained in VGPR; maintenance was continued. Peripheral neuropathy gradually improved under supportive therapy; blood counts recovered slowly but grade II cytopenia remained.
2025-01-24: Progressive disease (IgG 3034 mg/dL; bone marrow infiltration with 50% plasma cells). Lymphocyte apheresis (2025-02). Molecular genetics performed: gain 1q21, gain 17p13, one TP53 mutation detected. Bridging therapy with Isatuximab 10 mg/kg IV weekly, pomalidomide 3 mg PO d1–21 and dexamethasone 40 mg weekly. Restaging CT: no active or new extramedullary lesions. Urology evaluation: PSA slightly elevated above threshold, raising suspicion for synchronous prostate carcinoma. Organ function was largely preserved apart from known CKD (creatinine 1.6 mg/dL).
CAR-T therapy (2025-04-12): BCMA-directed CAR-T (ciltacabtagene autoleucel, Carvykti®) after lymphodepletion with fludarabine 15 mg/m² d−5 to d−3 and cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m² d−5 to d−3. Infusion was well tolerated, without ≥ grade 2 CRS or ICANS. 2025-04-30: The patient was discharged. Complete serological remission was achieved.
2025-05-12: The patient developed sepsis due to enteritis with Klebsiella pneumoniae bacteremia and concurrent Clostridioides difficile infection. Treated with meropenem and oral vancomycin. Persistent cytopenias required G-CSF support and eltrombopag 50 mg daily was started.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of current diagnoses and relapse type.
disease_status.content: MM, relapsed/refractory post CAR-T cells. Secondary MDS with TP53 mutation detected, no blast increase.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual information that influences treatment decisions, including comorbidities and risk scores.
special_considerations.content: 09/2025: The patient now presents with rrMM, with low tumor burden following BCMA-directed CAR-T therapy. Blood counts are low, EBV reactivation/infection was detected in the bone marrow. Bone marrow shows low-level infiltration with dysplastic features and focal blasts, consistent with secondary MDS with TP53 mutation (detected in CD138- fraction). Performance status is ECOG 1. Comorbidities include cardiomyopathy with moderately reduced ejection fraction, metabolic syndrome with impaired glucose control and a history of prostate carcinoma status post radical prostatectomy.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Cytopenia and new vertebral compression fractures in a heavily pretreated multiple myeloma patient in apparent biochemical remission, raising concern for therapy-related bone marrow failure or evolving secondary myeloid neoplasm.
differentials: Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome after multiple lines of myeloma therapy and high-dose melphalan
Relapsed or smoldering multiple myeloma with high bone marrow infiltration
Post-sepsis or post-CAR-T bone marrow suppression

treatment_plan.action: Perform bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to assess plasma-cell burden, dysplasia, and blast percentage.
Order MRI spine to evaluate vertebral compression fractures and exclude spinal cord compromise.
Obtain myeloma staging workup including immunoglobulins and serum free light chains to assess current disease status.
Arrange orthopedic consultation for assessment of vertebral stability and mobilization guidance.
Provide transfusion support for symptomatic anemia and thrombocytopenia as needed.

key_findings_quotes: He reported increasing fatigue over the preceding several weeks but remained able to perform daily activities.
He described pallor and noted worsening cytopenia on recent laboratory tests.
Hb 8.1 g/dL, WBC 3.2 ×10⁹/L, Plt 33 ×10⁹/L.
CT skeleton showed several osteolytic foci persisting in the thoracic spine and pelvis, consistent with known myeloma-related bone disease.
New vertebral compression fractures were observed at T7, T9, and T11, with up to 30% loss of vertebral body height.
Echocardiography demonstrated dilated cardiomyopathy with mildly reduced systolic function (LVEF 46%).

round_2.working_diagnosis: Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome with complex high-risk cytogenetics and low-burden residual multiple myeloma, with EBV reactivation in bone marrow.
differentials: Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome after prior myeloma therapy
Low-level relapsed multiple myeloma with concurrent EBV-driven cytopenia

treatment_plan.action: Perform molecular panel on bone marrow to identify mutations including TP53, FLT3, NPM1, and IDH for risk stratification.
Document that multiple myeloma persists at low burden with residual monotypic plasma cells (<5%) and a trace IgG-kappa band.
Recognize EBV infection in bone marrow (EBV DNA positive 3920 copies/mL) as a potential contributor to cytopenias and consider rituximab therapy.
Allow physiotherapeutic mobilization given MRI showing no spinal stenosis, epidural mass, or significant retropulsion.
Continue supportive care with transfusions while awaiting molecular results for definitive risk stratification.

key_findings_quotes: The aspirate smear was hypocellular for age (15%), showing markedly reduced trilineage hematopoiesis with dysplastic features in both erythroid and granulocytic lineages.
A blast population (4%) was detected, CD34+, CD117+, HLA-DR+, CD13+, CD33+, and negative for lymphoid markers (CD3-, CD19-, CD4-, CD8-, CD20-).
FISH: Showed gain of 1q and 5p, trisomy 3, tetrasomy 9 and 11, gain of 8q, and deletions of 17p and 5q.
Virology from bone-marrow aspirate: EBV DNA was positive (3 920 copies/mL).
Plasma cells were slightly increased, with a small atypical population (< 5%).

round_3.working_diagnosis: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (rrMM) and myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with TP53 mutation, post-chemotherapy (pCT), WHO 2022; IPSS-M 2.92 (very high risk), IPSS-R 8.0 (very high risk); not eligible for allogeneic transplantation due to age and comorbidities (HCT-CI 6-7 points).
differentials: Secondary/treatment-related MDS post-CAR-T cell therapy with TP53 mutation and relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma
TP53-mutated MDS-pCT with residual myeloma clone after CAR-T

treatment_plan.action: Treat EBV infection with rituximab 375 mg/m2 weekly for up to four doses to assess potential improvement of cytopenias.
Provide supportive care for MDS without disease-directed therapy given low blast count; no allogeneic stem cell transplantation is feasible due to age and comorbidities.
Defer myeloma-specific therapy given current low tumor burden; in case of progression, consider GPRC5D-targeted bispecific antibody.
Arrange psychiatric co-management for anxiety disorder and history of severe depressive episode.

key_findings_quotes: Final diagnosis (WHO 2022): Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (rrMM) and myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with TP53 mutation, post-chemotherapy (pCT).
CD138 negative fraction: Mutations in TP53 (1.: exon 4, c.273G>A, p.Trp91*(VAF 6%); 2.: exon 8, c.893_901delinsT, p.Glu298Valfs*5 (VAF 18%)).
IPSS-M: 2.92 (very high risk). IPSS-R: 8.0 (very high risk).
HCT-CI: 6 points (7 points age-adjusted).
Treat EBV infection with rituximab 375 mg/m2 weekly, up to a maximum of 4 doses, to assess potential improvement of cytopenia.
Multiple myeloma: Currently no therapy required. In case of progression, consider GPRC5D-targeted bispecific antibody.
MDS: No MDS-specific therapy recommended due to low blast count. Supportive care.
Allogeneic stem cell transplantation is not feasible given comorbidities and advanced patient age.
